sentence: If it rains tomorrow, we will stay at home
hint: First conditional: If + present, will + base verb
explanation: First conditional for real possibilities: If + present simple, will + base form.

sentence: I would travel the world if I had more money
hint: Second conditional: would + base verb, if + past simple
explanation: Second conditional for hypothetical: would + base (not 'will'). If + past simple.

sentence: If you study hard, you will pass the exam
explanation: First conditional: real possibility. If + present, will + base.

sentence: She would buy a house if she earned more
explanation: Second conditional: hypothetical. 'Would' (not 'will') + if + past simple.

sentence: If we leave now, we will arrive on time
explanation: First conditional: real possibility about leaving now.

sentence: If I were you, I would accept the job offer
hint: Use 'were' (not 'was') in second conditional with I/he/she
explanation: In formal second conditional, use 'were' for all subjects: 'If I were you'.

sentence: We will miss the bus if we do not hurry
explanation: First conditional with result clause first: 'We will miss...if we don't hurry'.

sentence: If he spoke better English, he would get the promotion
explanation: Second conditional: If + past simple ('spoke'), would + base form.

sentence: If the weather is nice, we will have a picnic
explanation: First conditional: If + present simple, will + base form.

sentence: I would learn to play the piano if I had time
explanation: Second conditional with result clause first.

sentence: If she calls me, I will tell her the news
explanation: First conditional: If + present ('calls'), will + base ('tell').

sentence: They would move to the countryside if they could
explanation: Second conditional: 'would move' + 'if they could' (not 'can' — past form needed).

sentence: If you do not water the plants, they will die
explanation: First conditional with negative if-clause.

sentence: If I lived near the beach, I would swim every day
explanation: Second conditional: If + past ('lived'), would + base ('swim').

sentence: We will go to the park if it stops raining
explanation: First conditional with result clause first.

sentence: If they offered me the position, I would take it
explanation: Second conditional: If + past ('offered'), would + base ('take').

sentence: If you eat too much, you will feel sick
explanation: First conditional for general consequences.

sentence: She would be happier if she changed her job
explanation: Second conditional: 'would be' + if + past ('changed').

sentence: If I see him at the meeting, I will give him your message
explanation: First conditional: If + present ('see'), will + base ('give').

sentence: If we had a bigger house, we would invite more friends
explanation: Second conditional: If + past ('had'), would + base ('invite').
